outcome_contract.why: Drift between near-duplicate screens is compounding silently; attribution data is already missing from apartment bookings, and visual inconsistencies are live. Fixing now prevents further compounding before the next feature layer touches these screens.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Both booking screens use the canonical analytics pattern (attribution present on all handoffs); visual tokens are consistent; double-room has component-level tests matching apartment coverage.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Fix 8 CSS class changes on NR card, 2 badge tokens, 3 dark mode borders
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Post-build QA loop required; design intent confirmed in acceptance

engineering_coverage.applicable: Migrate apartment to `trackThenNavigate` + attribution; field parity verified; null guard required
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Attribution visible in GA4 after deploy

engineering_coverage.applicable: Update 2 apartment test files; add double-room test suite
GA4 event payload field parity check at build time
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Existing fields must be preserved; new attribution fields added

engineering_coverage.applicable: Beacon transport confirmed equivalent; navigation callback pattern unchanged
All 3 tasks rollbackable independently via git revert

stage_payload.summary: Fix confirmed drift divergences between `ApartmentBookContent.tsx` and `DoubleRoomBookContent.tsx`. Three independent tasks: (1) migrate apartment analytics from `fireHandoffToEngine` to `trackThenNavigate` + `readAttribution` to restore attribution data on apartment handoff events; (2) correct visual token drift on NR card, saving badges, and dark mode borders; (3) add component-level tests for `DoubleRoomBookContent`. All tasks are independently rollbackable with no cross-task dependencies.
goals: Restore attribution data on apartment booking handoff events (currently all absent).
Fix live visual inconsistencies: NR card hover tokens, saving badge text, dark mode borders.
Close double-room test coverage gap (currently zero component-level tests).
non_goals: Unifying pax handling, rate codes, or room-specific copy.
Adding a CI-level duplicate screen detector.
Removing `fireHandoffToEngine` from `ga4-events.ts` (dead code cleanup is out of scope).
constraints: Existing GA4 event fields must be preserved on migration — only the call pattern changes.
`fireHandoffToEngine` in `ga4-events.ts` must remain (becomes dead code after migration; cleanup deferred).
Tests run in CI only — do not run Jest locally; push and use `gh run watch`.
assumptions: `trackThenNavigate` + `readAttribution` is the canonical analytics pattern going forward.
NR button using `brand-primary` (matching double-room) is the correct token — confirmed by double-room reference and internal flex/NR consistency.
`isLongStay` is apartment-specific; correct to be absent from double-room.
selected_approach: What was chosen: Fix-in-place — three targeted IMPLEMENT tasks, each scoped to one divergence type.
Why planning is not reopening option selection: Analysis was decisive; all three options compared; B and C eliminated with concrete rationale. No operator-only fork remained.

task_briefs.title: Migrate apartment analytics to canonical `trackThenNavigate` + attribution pattern
